methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Defense & Security (found 0 same-stage, 0 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 20 same-sector peers exist in Defense & Security, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in Defense & Dual-use
Public-co frontier-tech comp in Defense & Dual-use

data.narrative:
Baykar is a private-stage defense and security company founded by Özdemir Bayraktar. The comparable universe consists of 2 public companies (DRSHF, IDEAFORGE.NS) and 8 private peers including Anduril Industries, Saronic, Flock Safety, Shield AI, and Chaos Industries—a total of 10 comparable firms within the Defense & Security sector.

Peer-median valuation analysis proved inapplicable to this engagement. Of the 20 same-sector peers identified, filtering for matching stage, adjacent stage, or similar valuation tier yielded fewer than three datapoints in any subcategory. A forced all-sector median would introduce material bias given the structural differences between defense contractors and adjacent technology verticals. Consequently, no triangulated valuation range can be responsibly derived from comparable company multiples at this time.

The current rumored valuation of approximately $10 billion lacks sufficient anchoring data from the comparable set to validate or challenge. Material uncertainties preventing reliable triangulation include the absence of standardized financial disclosure for private defense firms, heterogeneity in product mix and customer concentration across the peer group, and the limited public trading reference points in the sector. A defensible valuation estimate for Baykar would require either expanded access to audited financials from private peers or primary transaction data from recent capital raises within the defense technology cohort.
